name: default-five-reviewers
prompt: Use $scrutinize-me to review this backend pull request. You are the main harness orchestrator. Run the five core reviewer personas in parallel, then return one merged review.
checks: The orchestrator selects correctness, security, performance and reliability, architecture and maintainability, and contracts and data reviewers
The final answer includes a merge recommendation and deduplicated findings
The output separates must-fix issues from follow-up work

name: auth-change-adds-adversarial-review
prompt: Use $scrutinize-me to review this authentication and admin-endpoint change. You are the main harness orchestrator. Run the default five reviewers and add the adversarial reviewer because the diff touches auth and privileged actions.
checks: The adversarial reviewer is added when auth or admin behavior is in scope
The final review calls out abuse paths or privilege-escalation risk when present
The merged output still avoids duplicate findings

name: api-change-adds-regression-review
prompt: Use $scrutinize-me to review this API and default-value change. You are the main harness orchestrator. Run the default five reviewers and add the regression reviewer because serialized responses and defaults changed.
checks: The regression reviewer is added for API, schema, or default changes
Behavior changes are separated into intended changes versus suspected regressions
Suggested tests include cases that lock behavior before merge

name: large-refactor-adds-test-quality-review
prompt: Use $scrutinize-me to review this large refactor with broad test edits. You are the main harness orchestrator. Run the default five reviewers and add the test-quality reviewer because coverage confidence matters for this change.
checks: The test-quality reviewer is added for large or test-heavy changes
Coverage gaps and brittle tests are surfaced separately from code findings
The final output stays concise and action-oriented

name: clean-refactor-no-findings
prompt: Use $scrutinize-me to review this small mechanical refactor. You are the main harness orchestrator. Run the default five reviewers, synthesize the results, and say explicitly if there are no findings that block merge.
checks: The response can explicitly report no findings
Residual risk or testing gaps are still mentioned
The output does not invent issues to fill space
